name: GR 92: Platja del Torn and Punta de la Rojala Loop
name_original: La Rojala & playa del Torn - Circular por la costa Daurada
description: Hike the easy 4.1-mile La Rojala & Playa del Torn circular route along the Costa Daurada, featuring beaches, cliffs, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal beauty with mountainous terrain. The region features a rugged coastline with natural harbors and coves, alongside inland areas characterized by olive groves and slopes covered in rosemary and sage. Trails ascend through mountains like the Sierra de Portella and Mola Nadell, providing extensive vistas of both the coast and the interior. The area also includes charming valleys and rural routes exploring historical elements and inland villages. Best hiking trails around Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant The most popular hiking route is GR 92: Platja del Torn and Punta de la Rojala Loop, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) easy trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route offers views of beaches, cliffs, and the La Rojala Natural Protected Area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Portella - Circular from l'Hospitalet de l'Infant, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.5 km) path. This trail ascends through mountainous terrain, providing panoramic coastal views. Local hikers also love the Masia de Castelló – Masboquera Village loop from Masboquera, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through rural areas and a deserted village, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant is defined by dramatic coastlines, mountainous terrain, and charming valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 390 reviews. More than 2100 hikers have used komoot to explore Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant?
A: There are nearly 100 hiking trails in the region, offering a wide variety of experiences. You'll find options ranging from easy coastal strolls to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: The region boasts a diverse landscape, seamlessly blending a dramatic, rugged coastline with mountainous inland terrain. You can hike along paths offering spectacular sea views, through areas with olive groves and slopes covered in rosemary and sage, or explore charming valleys and rural routes with historical elements like terraced walls.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, the area offers many easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e954163537/gr-92-platja-del-torn-and-punta-de-la-rojala-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Platja del Torn and Punta de la Rojala Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1-mile trail with views of beaches and cliffs. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/991988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masia de Castelló – Masboquera Village loop from Masboquera&lt;/a&gt;, which explores rural areas and a deserted village.

Q: Where can I find trails with panoramic views?
A: For extensive vistas, consider trails that ascend into the mountains. The Sierra de Portella offers panoramic coastal views, and an ascent to Mola Nadell provides broad vistas of both the coast and the inland mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala de Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e954163537/gr-92-platja-del-torn-and-punta-de-la-rojala-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Platja del Torn and Punta de la Rojala Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases beaches and cliffs, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951872543/sierra-de-portella-circular-from-l-hospitalet-de-l-infant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Portella - Circular from l'Hospitalet de l'Infant&lt;/a&gt; for a more challenging mountain experience.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I discover along the trails?
A: Hikers can explore a variety of landmarks. The GR-92 coastal path reveals rugged cliffs and natural harbors. Inland, you might encounter historical elements like skillfully terraced walls and ruins of old corrals. Notable natural features include the La Rojala Natural Protected Area with its viewpoint, and the deserted village of Masia de Castelló. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Roig Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2558939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rifà Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant?
A: The region is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. Even in winter, the mild Mediterranean climate can allow for enjoyable hikes, especially along the coast. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951872543/sierra-de-portella-circular-from-l-hospitalet-de-l-infant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Portella - Circular from l'Hospitalet de l'Infant&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 13.4-mile path through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain. Some trails in the region may even involve short climbing sections secured with chains and ropes, requiring good physical condition and sure-footedness.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant?
A: The trails in Vandellòs I L'Hospitalet De L'Infant are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 390 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning diversity of landscapes, from the dramatic coastal views to the tranquil inland valleys, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Can I find trails that connect different beaches or coastal areas?
A: Yes, the region's coastline offers paths that link beautiful beaches and coves. The GR-92, also known as the Mediterranean Path, runs along the entire Catalan coast, providing diverse sections with sea views. For example, the El Jonquet Coastal Path is a low-difficulty natural trail connecting two Blue Flag beaches, Platja de l'Arenal and Platja del Torn.

Q: Are there any routes that explore traditional villages or rural history?
A: Yes, some routes delve into the region's rural heritage. The Vall de Llors, for instance, offers trails that explore the interplay between mountains and the sea, incorporating historical elements and leading to inland villages such as Masboquera and Masriudoms. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/991988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masia de Castelló – Masboquera Village loop from Masboquera&lt;/a&gt; specifically visits a deserted village.
tips: Attention, nudist beach! We were able to hike on the heights and with the bad weather there was almost no one, but in summer we must come across a lot of naked people ;)
Mountain more than 150 meters high that gives a panoramic view of the Costa Daurada.
Small cove that has the name of Cap de Bea and that separates this cove from Platja de l'Arenal

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike usually takes around 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 6.6 km route, but your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend enjoying the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered easy and suitable for most fitness levels, including families. It's also dog-friendly, making it a great option for a family outing with your furry companion.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start the hike?
faqs.answer: You can start the route from the outskirts of L'Hospitalet de l'Infant or near the Templo del Sol naturist campsite. Both locations offer convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter mostly natural paths (about 3.66 km), unpaved sections (2.37 km), and some flat stones (497 m), with very little asphalt. While generally easy, some areas along the Costa Daurada can involve steep downhill sections and even light climbing for more advanced hikers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers magnificent panoramic views of the Costa Daurada coastline. Key highlights include the beautiful Platja del Torn, known for its fine sand and transparent waters, and Punta de la Rojala, which is the highest point and offers expansive coastal views. You'll also pass by Cala Bea and the El Torn Islet with its 16th-century fortification.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You'll find parts of the Catalunya E28, GR-92, Camí de la Cantera de RENFE, and Passeig de l'Arenal integrated into the circular path.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Costa Daurada generally offers pleasant weather for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal for comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon hike is recommended if visiting during peak season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this natural area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. The area, known as La Rojala-Platja del Torn, is a protected natural reserve and part of the European Natura 2000 Network, emphasizing its significant landscape and biodiversity value, but it remains freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is Platja del Torn suitable for swimming?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Platja del Torn is renowned for its fine sand and transparent waters, making it an excellent spot for swimming. It's a natural beach surrounded by pine trees and cliffs, and has received certifications like the Blue Flag, indicating high environmental quality.

faqs.question: What makes Platja del Torn unique?
faqs.answer: Platja del Torn is a Protected Nature Reserve recognized for its unique landscape and long-standing naturist tradition. It's home to El Templo del Sol, a naturist campsite, and boasts certifications like the Blue Flag and ISO 14001, highlighting its environmental quality and services.
